notes: New skill (with_skill) achieved 100% pass rate across all 3 evals (14/14 expectations passed)
Old skill (old_skill) achieved 93% pass rate (13/14 expectations passed)
The one failure in old_skill: eval-1 'Defines a Protocol for the client' — old skill's Protocol guidance was present but less prominent, leading to TYPE_CHECKING imports being used instead
Eval-2 (pydantic-vs-dataclass) was a tie — both versions handle the validation boundary pattern equally well since it's well-covered in both
Eval-3 (order-processing) was a tie on pass rate, but new skill response also applied Protocol for Notifier interface (not just singledispatch), showing broader pattern adoption
New skill's before/after examples for validation boundary and Protocol vs ABC appear to have improved pattern adoption
New skill is 400 lines vs 537 lines (25% reduction), making it more focused and within the 500-line guideline
